projectDescription: A competitive open-source chess engine written in Rust, using NNUE evaluation and advanced alpha-beta search, designed to interface with UCI-compatible chess GUIs.
keyFeatures: NNUE evaluation with 3-layer network (L1=768, L2=16, L3=32) and AVX-512/AVX2/NEON SIMD acceleration
Alpha-beta search with iterative deepening, aspiration windows, and principal variation search (PVS)
Advanced pruning: Null Move Pruning, Reverse Futility Pruning, Razoring, ProbCut, Late Move Pruning
Singular extensions with double and triple extension support
Late Move Reductions (LMR) with history-based adjustments
Multi-threaded search with NUMA-aware thread pool (up to 512 threads)
Syzygy endgame tablebase support via Fathom library
Transposition table with aging and cluster-based replacement policy
publicApiSurface: setoption name <name> value <value> — configure engine option
ucinewgame — reset engine state for new game
perft <depth> — count leaf nodes at given depth
eval — print NNUE evaluation of current position
d — display current board position and FEN
compiler — print compiler version and build flags
UCI option: SyzygyPath (string, requires syzygy feature)
